customer_anxiety_profile: Process-skeptical. Janet has been through the solar lead-gen funnel before and feels misled by 'instant code' promises that led to home-visit pitches. She is cooperative but on a show-me basis. Her engagement is conditional on the tax-credit timeline being real.
unresolved_concerns: Janet's $4.34 average monthly bill is almost certainly a data error — possibly a daily rate, a partial billing period, or a misread. The closer must re-establish the actual bill before building any economic case.
Michael Dewar is on the title and is not on the appointment. Janet described his schedule as unpredictable. The closer should attempt to include Michael before or at the start of the appointment.

Janet's prior experience with solar lead-gen (broken 'instant code' promises, unexpected home-visit requirements) was never addressed on the setter call. The closer should expect residual skepticism about process and differentiate Ion's approach early.
sensitive_topics_raised: Tax-credit installation deadline — Janet asked directly whether Ion can install before the credit expires. Carter said yes. The closer must be prepared to address timeline specifics.
Prior negative solar lead-gen experiences — Janet disclosed frustration with the 'instant code' funnel and unexpected home-visit requirements.
